sourceTitle: Give your to-dos a Home with Task databases

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the My Tasks widget in Notion Home?

options.text: To display a list of all pages you have recently visited
options.explanation: Recent pages are shown by the Recently visited widget, not My Tasks.

options.text: To consolidate every task assigned to you from any Task database in the workspace
options.explanation: My Tasks pulls together all tasks assigned to you from any Task database, giving you a single to‑do list.

options.text: To suggest articles and guides based on your activity
options.explanation: Suggested for you widget provides reading suggestions; My Tasks does not.

options.text: To let you create new databases directly from Home
options.explanation: You can add new tasks, but creating entire databases is not a function of My Tasks.

questions.question: Which three properties must exist in a database before you can turn it into a Task database?

options.text: Status, Assignee, and Due date
options.explanation: These three properties are required for Notion to treat the database as a Task database.

options.text: Title, Tags, and Created time
options.explanation: While useful, these are not the required properties for a Task database.

options.text: Priority, Project, and Owner
options.explanation: These are optional fields; they are not mandatory for conversion.

options.text: Completed, Comments, and Attachments
options.explanation: These are not the three required properties listed in the guide.

questions.question: If you want to see tasks that are due in the next week, which feature should you modify?

options.text: The widget’s color scheme
options.explanation: Changing colors does not affect which tasks are displayed.

options.text: The default sort order of the database
options.explanation: Sorting changes the order but not the filter criteria.

options.text: The filter settings of the My Tasks view
options.explanation: Filters let you limit tasks to those due within a specific timeframe, such as the next week.

options.text: The page’s sidebar width
options.explanation: Sidebar width is unrelated to task visibility.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes the real‑time sync behavior of the My Tasks widget?

options.text: It updates only when you manually refresh the Home page.
options.explanation: The widget syncs automatically; manual refresh is not required.

options.text: It reflects changes (new assignments, status updates) instantly across all Task databases.
options.explanation: The guide states the widget is synced in real time, so any change appears immediately.

options.text: It syncs once per hour to reduce server load.
options.explanation: There is no hourly delay mentioned; sync is immediate.

options.text: It only syncs tasks that are marked as high priority.
options.explanation: All assigned tasks sync, regardless of priority.

questions.question: You have a Task database that lacks a Status property. What is the correct order of steps to make My Tasks display items from this database?

options.text: Add a Status property, then turn the database into a Task database via Settings … → Turn into task database.
options.explanation: The guide instructs you to add missing required properties before converting the database.

options.text: Convert the database into a Task database first, then add the missing Status property.
options.explanation: Conversion will fail without the required properties; they must exist first.

options.text: Rename the database to ‘My Tasks’ and it will automatically work.
options.explanation: Renaming does not add required properties or enable task functionality.

options.text: Duplicate the database, add the Status property to the copy, and delete the original.
options.explanation: Duplicating is unnecessary; you can add the property directly to the existing database.

questions.question: Which view types can you select for the full‑page My Tasks database?

options.text: Kanban board, gallery, timeline, calendar, and list
options.explanation: The article lists all these layouts as available options for My Tasks.

options.text: Spreadsheet, mind map, and Gantt chart
options.explanation: These view types are not mentioned as options for My Tasks.

options.text: Table, board, and calendar only
options.explanation: While table is a default view, the guide explicitly mentions gallery and timeline as well.

options.text: Presentation mode and dark mode
options.explanation: These are display settings, not view layouts for the database.

questions.question: If you want to see tasks assigned to any member of your team rather than just yourself, what should you do?

options.text: Change the filter to include tasks where Assignee is not empty.
options.explanation: Adjusting the filter to remove the personal Assignee condition lets you view tasks assigned to anyone.

options.text: Rename the widget to ‘Team Tasks’.
options.explanation: Renaming does not affect the underlying filter logic.

options.text: Add a new property called ‘Team’ and manually tag each task.
options.explanation: While possible, the guide suggests simply adjusting the existing filter rather than adding a new property.

options.text: Switch the widget to ‘Trending’ mode.
options.explanation: Trending shows popular pages, not team‑assigned tasks.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a widget that can appear on Notion Home alongside My Tasks?

options.text: Recently visited
options.explanation: Recently visited is explicitly mentioned as a Home widget.

options.text: Suggested for you
options.explanation: Suggested for you is also listed among Home widgets.

options.text: Trending topics
options.explanation: Trending topics are part of Home’s widget set.

options.text: Project timeline overview
options.explanation: The guide does not mention a ‘Project timeline overview’ widget; it focuses on recent pages, suggestions, trending, templates, and learning.

questions.question: What advantage does adding a new task directly from the My Tasks widget provide?

options.text: It bypasses all required properties, letting you create a task with only a title.
options.explanation: The guide notes that some properties are automatically filled, but required properties still apply.

options.text: It automatically assigns the task to you and sets the default status based on your database settings.
options.explanation: When you create a task from My Tasks, Notion pre‑populates fields like Assignee and Status according to the source database’s defaults.

options.text: It creates the task in a hidden sandbox database that only you can see.
options.explanation: Tasks are added to the underlying Task database, not a hidden sandbox.

options.text: It instantly shares the new task with every member of the workspace.
options.explanation: Sharing depends on the Assignee or permissions; creating a task does not automatically share it with everyone.

questions.question: How can you change the default page that appears when you open Notion Home?

options.text: By selecting a different widget layout in the Home settings menu.
options.explanation: Widget layout does not control the default landing page.

options.text: By opening the settings menu (…) at the top right and choosing either ‘Last visited page’ or ‘First page in sidebar’.
options.explanation: The article explains this exact method for setting the default Home page.

options.text: By dragging a page into the Home area and renaming it ‘Home’.
options.explanation: Renaming does not affect the default opening behavior.

options.text: By disabling the Home feature in your account preferences.
options.explanation: Home cannot be disabled via account preferences; it can only be customized.
